title: BjÃ¶rn Hartmann | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: BjÃ¶rn Hartmann | EECS at UC Berkeley BjÃ¶rn Hartmann: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo BjÃ¶rn Hartmann Associate Professor Contact Information 417 Sutardja Dai Hall bjoern@eecs.berkeley.edu Office Hours https://calendly.com/bjoern-ucb, 417 Sutardja Dai Links Personal Homepage Publications Dissertations Research Support Amy Frithsen frithsen@berkeley.edu Biography BjÃ¶rn Hartmann is an Associate Professor in EECS. He received a BA in Communication, a BSE in Digital Media Design, and an MSE in Computer and Information Science from the University of Pennsylvania in 2002. He received his PhD degree in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2009. His research in Human-Computer Interaction focuses on the creation and evaluation of user interface design tools, end-user programming environments, and crowdsourcing systems. BjÃ¶rn received an Okawa Research Grant and an NSF CAREER Award in 2012, and a Sloan Research Fellowship in 2013. Education 2009, Ph.D., Computer Science, Stanford University 2002, MSE, Computer and Information Science, University of Pennsylvania 2001, BSE/B.A., Digital Media Design/Communication, University of Pennsylvania Research Areas Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) Artificial Intelligence (AI) Programming Systems (PS) Graphics (GR) Research Centers Berkeley Center for New Media (BCNM) Berkeley Institute of Design (BID) Center for Information Technology Research in the Interest of Society - The Banatao Institute (CITRIS) EPIC Data lab (EPIC) Jacobs Institute for Design Innovation (JIDI) Teaching Schedule CS 294-304.
